summary: Sprint 9 significantly improves maintainability. analytics.py reduced from 762L to 16L — a clear improvement. portfolios.py reduced from 751L to 170L by splitting holdings and transactions to their own modules. dashboard/page.tsx reduced from 603L to ~280L. All new TypeScript components have explicit interfaces. Python modules follow PEP 8 with type annotations. The _assert_portfolio_owner duplication across 3 modules is the main maintainability concern but is a reasonable trade-off for avoiding circular imports. The DashboardMetrics.tsx file at 225 lines is within guidelines. The PortfolioList.tsx at 136 lines is well within guidelines.

findings.title: _assert_portfolio_owner duplicated in 3 backend modules
findings.detail: The _assert_portfolio_owner and _assert_holding_owner helpers are duplicated. This is the expected outcome of a code split without a shared utils module. Medium severity because the logic is simple and unlikely to change.
findings.fix: Create backend/app/api/portfolio_utils.py with shared helpers and import across all 3 modules in the next refactor sprint.

findings.title: WidgetErrorFallback duplicated in page.tsx and PortfolioList.tsx
findings.detail: The WidgetErrorFallback component is defined in both page.tsx and PortfolioList.tsx with identical logic.
findings.fix: Extract to a shared component file, e.g. frontend/src/components/WidgetErrorFallback.tsx.
